decks.notes: This deck focuses on controlling the game by countering opponent's spells, removing threats, and drawing cards. The goal is to outvalue the opponent and establish a dominant board presence through card advantage and efficient removal spells. Teferi, Hero of Dominaria and Teferi, Time Raveler are key cards in this strategy, as they provide card advantage and disruption while also having powerful abilities. The deck aims to control the game until it can win through incremental advantage and powerful finishers like Wan Shi Tong, Librarian and Wrath of the Skies.

The deck focuses on generating explosive mana through artifacts while also leveraging Emry's recursion ability. Tamiyo and Quantum Riddler help with card advantage and selection, while Cori-Steel Cutter and Mishra's Bauble provide additional synergy with artifacts. The deck aims to overwhelm opponents with cheap threats like Unholy Heat and Metallic Rebuke, while also utilizing Urza's Saga to find key artifacts. The inclusion of burn spells like Lightning Bolt and Flame of Anor provide additional reach to close out games quickly. Overall, the deck aims to be fast, aggressive, and resourceful in its gameplay.

The deck revolves around quickly reanimating powerful creatures like Griselbrand and Superior Spider-Man using cards like Goryo's Vengeance and Ephemerate, while also disrupting opponents with instant spells like Force of Negation and Prismatic Ending. Atraxa, Grand Unifier provides support and protection for the combo pieces, ensuring a strong and successful reanimation strategy. The deck also contains disruptive spells like Thoughtseize and Teferi, Time Raveler to control the game and disrupt opponents' plans. The mana base is designed to support the various colored spells and ensure consistent casting of the combo pieces.

The deck focuses on controlling the game through the use of powerful Eldrazi creatures and disruptive spells. By utilizing the UrzaTron lands to ramp up mana production, the deck can quickly cast its high-cost threats like Ulamog, the Ceaseless Hunger and Thought-Knot Seer. The deck also uses Karn, the Great Creator to search for specific artifacts to disrupt opponents further. With a combination of hand disruption, removal spells, and big threats, the deck aims to dominate the board and outvalue opponents in the long game.

This Boros Aggro deck focuses on using fast and aggressive creatures to overwhelm the opponent quickly. The key strategy is to play efficient creatures like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Ocelot Pride early on, supported by removal spells like Galvanic Discharge and Lightning Bolt. The deck also includes cards like Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and Ranger-Captain of Eos for additional value and card advantage. By maintaining pressure on the opponent and utilizing efficient creature removal, the deck aims to win the game before the opponent can stabilize.

The deck focuses on using creatures like Basking Broodscale and Glaring Fleshraker to generate tokens and control the board, while also utilizing cards like Writhing Chrysalis and Emrakul, the Promised End for powerful combo plays. The deck also includes various support cards like Blade of the Bloodchief and Kozilek's Command to help maintain control over the battlefield. Urza's Saga is used to tutor for key combo pieces, while the land base includes cards like Eldrazi Temple and Cavern of Souls to support the deck's strategy. Overall, the deck aims to overwhelm opponents with a powerful and synergistic combo strategy.

The key concept of this deck is to quickly fill the graveyard with powerful creatures and then bring them back onto the battlefield using reanimation spells like Unearth and Persist. The deck uses a combination of cheap removal and disruption spells like Thoughtseize and Fatal Push to control the game while setting up the combo. The critical creatures like Archon of Cruelty and Emperor of Bones provide immediate impact on the board when they are brought back, overwhelming the opponent and securing victory. The mana base is optimized for consistency and speed in executing the combo.

The main strategy of the deck is to control the game by disrupting the opponent's resources with cards like Thought-Knot Seer and Chalice of the Void, while ramping up to play powerful threats like Karn, the Great Creator and Ugin, the Spirit Dragon. The deck utilizes the UrzaTron lands to generate a large amount of mana quickly, allowing for the deck to play its high-cost spells ahead of schedule. The deck aims to dominate the late game by overwhelming the opponent with powerful Eldrazi creatures and colorless spells.

The deck aims to flood the board with cheap artifacts and utilize synergy between them to overwhelm the opponent quickly. It includes a mix of creatures, artifacts, and support spells to create a fast and aggressive playstyle. The key strategy is to utilize cards like Kappa Cannoneer and Arcbound Ravager to grow threats while also disrupting opponents with cards like Engineered Explosives and Tormod's Crypt. The deck also has some controlling elements with cards like Metallic Rebuke and Sink into Stupor to protect its board and disrupt the opponent's game plan.

The deck focuses on assembling mana-producing lands like Simic Growth Chamber and Amulet of Vigor to ramp into powerful threats like Primeval Titan and Cultivator Colossus. It utilizes cards like Green Sun's Zenith and Urza's Saga to search for key pieces, while also including disruption like Malevolent Rumble and Spelunking to protect the combo. The deck aims to quickly set up an overwhelming board presence and overwhelm the opponent with big threats and land-based interactions.

This deck focuses on using Amulet of Vigor to generate mana quickly and efficiently, allowing the player to ramp up to powerful threats like Primeval Titan and Cultivator Colossus. With cards like Green Sun's Zenith and Scapeshift to search for key pieces, as well as the land manipulation of Urza's Saga and Gruul Turf, the deck aims to assemble combos that can quickly overwhelm the opponent. The inclusion of spells like Malevolent Rumble and Aftermath Analyst adds versatility and disruption to the deck, making it a potent force in Modern 1 vs 1 gameplay.

The deck focuses on quickly filling up the graveyard with cards like Stinkweed Imp, Golgari Thug, and Faithless Looting in order to bring back Arclight Phoenix and Creeping Chill from the graveyard, while also maintaining card advantage with Life from the Loam and burning through the deck with Burning Inquiry. The deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with early game pressure and explosive plays, utilizing dredge mechanics and spells like Lava Dart and Otherworldly Gaze to maintain control and disrupt the opponent's strategy.

The main idea of this Boros Aggro deck is to overwhelm the opponent with a combination of aggressive creatures and efficient burn spells. By playing powerful threats like Ajani, Guide of Souls, and Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer, the deck aims to establish board presence early and consistently apply pressure. The inclusion of cards like Seasoned Pyromancer and Goblin Bombardment provides additional reach and removal options. Additionally, disruptive cards like Blood Moon and Static Prison can disrupt the opponent's game plan, while mana fixing tools like Arid Mesa and Flooded Strand ensure a smooth mana base.

Utilize a combination of land destruction, exile removal, and artifact control to disrupt opponents and control the game. Generate card advantage with Monumental Henge and outvalue opponents with Solitude and Price of Freedom. Use powerful effects like March of Otherworldly Light and Ark of Hunger to finish the game. Utilize Ensnaring Bridge to protect yourself from aggressive opponents and slow down the game. Overall, the deck aims to control the board and disrupt opponents' strategies while establishing and maintaining a powerful board presence.

The deck focuses on quickly enabling a powerful combo by utilizing instant reanimation cards like Goryo's Vengeance and Faithful Mending to bring back key creatures such as Griselbrand. The creatures in the deck, like Atraxa, Psychic Frog, and Quantum Riddler, are meant to control the board and provide additional value when reanimated. The inclusion of counterspells like Force of Negation and disruption cards like Thoughtseize helps protect the combo pieces. Teferi, Time Raveler adds an additional layer of control and consistency to the deck. The mana base is designed to provide flexibility and consistency in casting the necessary spells.

The focus of this deck is to quickly find and play Hydroelectric Specimen and Tameshi, Reality Architect to create a powerful combo with Goblin Charbelcher. By using a variety of counterspells and disruptive spells, along with card draw and tutoring effects, the deck aims to protect and assemble its combo pieces to win the game without relying on traditional land cards. The deck has multiple ways to search for key cards and disrupt opponents, making it difficult for opponents to stop the combo from going off.
